evidence: Multiple compatibility surfaces remain active in assigned files: `legacySupabaseFacade.ts`, `joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts`, `legacyStructureVideo.ts`, `taskIdHelpers.ts`, `galleryUtils.ts`, and `mediaTypeHelpers.ts`.
`handleError.ts` marks `reportRuntimeError` and `RuntimeErrorOptions` deprecated with removal target `2026-06-30`, but the alias is still actively used across runtime code paths.
`legacySupabaseFacade.ts` is deprecated with removal target `2026-06-30` and still has very high importer volume (packet context: 130 importers), indicating migration has not converged.

`joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` still carries broad legacy shape support, while non-test callsite search found no production imports of `createJoinClipsTaskCompat`, suggesting stale migration shim retention.

Legacy field fallbacks (`task_id`, `orchestrator_task_id`, `metadata.generation_id`, `start_image_url`, `input_image_paths_resolved`) remain accepted without explicit deprecation metadata or sunset policy in several helper modules.
holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles shows `src/shared/components/` has 70 files and mixed concerns (55 production + 15 test), indicating a flat overloaded package boundary.

holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles shows `src/shared/hooks/` has 81 production files while domain-specific alternatives also exist (`src/shared/settings/hooks/`, `src/shared/hooks/theme/`), indicating unresolved ownership split.
`src/shared/components/AuthGate.tsx` and `src/shared/auth/components/AuthGate.tsx` contain the same implementation, creating two authoritative paths for one auth boundary component.
`src/shared/components/AspectRatioVisualizer.tsx` and `src/shared/components/generation-controls/AspectRatioVisualizer.tsx` are duplicated, while consumers import from different locations.

`src/shared/hooks/useDebouncedSettingsSave.ts` and `src/shared/settings/hooks/useDebouncedSettingsSave.ts` are parallel implementations with parallel tests in both hook trees, showing incomplete package consolidation.

`src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/useFormSubmission.ts` and `.../formSubmission/useSubmissionHandlers.ts` repeatedly forward the same large state surface through multiple wrapper hooks before execution.

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/MediaLightbox.tsx` still exposes a very wide flat prop API and then re-bundles it (`useBundledLightboxProps`) rather than reducing the contract at the boundary; `.../hooks/useSharedLightboxState.ts` mirrors this with a very large composed input/return surface.

`src/shared/hooks/useTimelineCore.ts` and `src/shared/hooks/timeline/useTimelineFrameUpdates.ts` duplicate queue logging/write orchestration primitives (`shortId`, queued/start/end logging branches, serialized write plumbing), indicating parallel implementations of the same workflow.

Supabase functions in the same area use two different structural patterns: `initEdgeRuntime` + shared HTTP helpers (`generate-pat`, `revoke-pat`, `setup-auto-topup`, `complete-auto-topup-setup`) versus manual env/client/Response plumbing (`stripe-checkout`, `generate-upload-url`, `get-task-output`).

findings.summary: Deprecated facades remain first-class runtime APIs, so migration deadlines are declarative rather than enforced.
evidence: `legacySupabaseFacade.ts` exports deprecated `getLegacySupabaseClient()` with removal target `2026-06-30`, but behavior only warns after date and still returns client.
`handleError.ts` deprecates `reportRuntimeError`/`RuntimeErrorOptions` (same target date), but aliases remain exported and callable.
`useCredits.ts` is a deprecated compatibility re-export shim with no owner/removal metadata, allowing indefinite survival.
findings.suggestion: Move these to a single deprecation-governed contract: add explicit owner/remove-by metadata for every shim, emit telemetry on each deprecated call, enforce import budgets, and fail CI once budget reaches zero; then codemod callsites to canonical APIs.

findings.summary: Legacy payload keys are still normalized in core helpers without a bounded sunset path, preserving mixed schemas indefinitely.
evidence: `galleryUtils.ts` resolves both explicit legacy-like per-segment fields and orchestrator array fields in one read path.
`taskIdHelpers.ts` still prioritizes `source_task_id`, `orchestrator_task_id`, and legacy `task_id` with no deprecation gate.
`mediaTypeHelpers.ts` reads `generation_id`, `metadata.generation_id`, and `id` for compatibility with varied shapes.
`legacyStructureVideo.ts` defines deprecated structure-video fields but does not provide enforcement/removal gating mechanics in-module.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical read contract per domain and isolate legacy parsing to a narrow boundary adapter with removal policy + deadline checks; remove legacy keys from shared generic helpers once boundary adapters are in place.

findings.summary: Join-clips compat adapter appears test-only in production usage, but still carries large legacy branching surface.
evidence: `joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` includes many legacy path/video-edit branches and deprecated wrapper API.
Non-test callsite search found no production imports of `createJoinClipsTaskCompat` or `toCanonicalJoinClipsTaskInput` outside the adapter file itself.
Compatibility logic remains maintained despite apparent migration completion at runtime callsites.
findings.suggestion: If production callers are truly gone, remove the compat adapter and keep only canonical join-clips APIs; otherwise, reintroduce it as an explicit boundary module with a tracked importer allowlist and automatic expiration.

findings.summary: AuthGate exists in two packages with identical code, splitting ownership of auth boundary UI.
evidence: `src/shared/components/AuthGate.tsx` and `src/shared/auth/components/AuthGate.tsx` both export the same `AuthGate` implementation.
Both locations also have mirrored tests, confirming duplicate package roots rather than aliasing.
Runtime imports currently point to the components-root path (`src/app/providers/AppProviders.tsx`), leaving the auth package copy as parallel surface area.
findings.suggestion: Stage 1: choose `src/shared/auth/components/AuthGate.tsx` as canonical auth-boundary location. Stage 2: replace all imports from `@/shared/components/AuthGate` to `@/shared/auth/components/AuthGate` (`rg -n "shared/components/AuthGate" src && rg -l "shared/components/AuthGate" src | xargs sed -i '' 's@/shared/components/AuthGate@/shared/auth/components/AuthGate@g'`). Stage 3: delete the duplicate component and duplicate test under `src/shared/components/`. Stage 4: validate with `pnpm vitest src/shared/auth/components/__tests__/AuthGate.test.tsx` and `pnpm tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: AspectRatioVisualizer is duplicated across generic and generation-controls packages.
evidence: Both visualizer files have matching implementation and props, not a wrapper/re-export relationship.
Consumers import from both locations (`GenerationSettingsSection` uses shared root; `AspectRatioSelector` uses generation-controls), so package boundaries are not authoritative.
findings.suggestion: Stage 1: pick one owner package (recommended: `src/shared/components/generation-controls/AspectRatioVisualizer.tsx` because it is generation-settings specific). Stage 2: keep one implementation and convert the other path to a temporary re-export. Stage 3: migrate imports to the canonical path (`rg -n "AspectRatioVisualizer" src/shared/components src/tools`). Stage 4: remove temporary re-export after import count reaches zero. Validate with `pnpm vitest src/shared/components/generation-controls/AspectRatioSelector.test.tsx src/shared/components/__tests__/AspectRatioVisualizer.test.tsx` and `pnpm tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: Debounced settings save hook is maintained in both `shared/hooks` and `shared/settings/hooks` trees.
evidence: Two full hook implementations exist with same API and lifecycle behavior in parallel directories.
Two near-identical test suites exist, one per location, indicating migration fork instead of a single package owner.
`useAutoSaveSettings` also exists in both trees and each imports its local copy, reinforcing duplicated directory boundary.
findings.suggestion: Stage 1: declare `src/shared/settings/hooks/` as canonical settings autosave package and mark `src/shared/hooks/useDebouncedSettingsSave.ts` as temporary compatibility re-export. Stage 2: move all imports to `@/shared/settings/hooks/useDebouncedSettingsSave` and `@/shared/settings/hooks/useAutoSaveSettings` (`rg -n "shared/hooks/useDebouncedSettingsSave|shared/hooks/settings/useAutoSaveSettings" src`). Stage 3: consolidate to one test suite under `src/shared/settings/hooks/__tests__/`. Stage 4: delete duplicate files in `src/shared/hooks/` once importers are zero. Validate with `pnpm vitest src/shared/settings/hooks/__tests__/useDebouncedSettingsSave.test.ts` and `pnpm tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: Component tests are split between root colocated files and `__tests__`, weakening navigation conventions.
evidence: `BulkEditControls.test.tsx` is colocated in the already overloaded `src/shared/components/` root, while many sibling component tests are under `src/shared/components/__tests__/`.
directory profile for `src/shared/components/` reports 70 files with mixed production/test concerns, so inconsistent test placement increases root-level scanning cost.
findings.suggestion: Stage 1: standardize component-test placement to `src/shared/components/__tests__/` for root-level shared components. Stage 2: move `BulkEditControls.test.tsx` to `src/shared/components/__tests__/BulkEditControls.test.tsx` and update relative imports. Stage 3: run import/path verification (`pnpm vitest src/shared/components/__tests__/BulkEditControls.test.tsx`) and enforce with a lint rule or project convention doc. Stage 4: run `pnpm tsc --noEmit` to ensure no stale path references.

findings.summary: Generation/shot type ownership is split across two top-level packages with overlapping scope.
evidence: `src/types/generationAndShots.ts` is documented as canonical and re-exports `generationMetadata`, `generationParams`, `generationRow`, and `shot` from `src/types/*`.

The structure profile also contains `src/domains/generation/types/` with the same concern family (`generationMetadata.ts`, `generationParams.ts`, `generationRow.ts`, `shot.ts`), creating parallel package homes for one domain.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) set `src/domains/generation/types/` as the sole owner of generation/shot domain types; (2) move or alias `src/types/generationMetadata.ts`, `generationParams.ts`, `generationRow.ts`, `shot.ts` into domain types in that order; (3) update `src/types/generationAndShots.ts` to re-export from `@/domains/generation/types/*` as a temporary compatibility barrel; (4) migrate imports from `@/types/*` to `@/domains/generation/types/*`; (5) remove transitional aliases after usage reaches zero. Validation commands: `rg -n "@/types/(generationMetadata|generationParams|generationRow|shot)" src`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run check:contracts`.

findings.summary: Legacy Supabase facade remains a central dependency instead of a quarantined migration edge.
evidence: `legacySupabaseFacade.ts` is marked deprecated with removal target `2026-06-30`, but holistic structure shows `src/integrations/supabase/legacy/` avg_fan_in 65.5.

Directory importers include many active runtime areas (`src/shared/hooks/` = 34 imports and multiple tool/component directories), which indicates migration compatibility code is still in normal feature dependency paths.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) introduce a narrow transitional package `src/integrations/supabase/compat/` that is allowed to import `legacy/`; (2) move active feature imports to runtime entrypoints (`@/integrations/supabase/runtime/...`) first, leaving only compat wrappers; (3) restrict direct imports of `legacySupabaseFacade` to compat package via lint/check rule; (4) remove `legacy/` once budget reaches zero. Validation commands: `rg -n "legacySupabaseFacade|@/integrations/supabase/legacy" src`, `npm run check:legacy-supabase-usage`, `npm run quality:check`.

findings.summary: Documentation and runtime files expose different policy ownership paths for Supabase legacy governance.
evidence: README states Supabase legacy shim policy is sourced from `src/shared/lib/governance/deprecationPolicy.ts`.

The runtime facade imports policy symbols from `src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabasePolicy.ts`, which itself wraps governance policy. This creates a two-home ownership signal (governance vs legacy package) without explicit package boundary guidance.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) declare one policy ownership folder in docs (`src/shared/lib/governance/` as source-of-truth, `legacy/` as consumer); (2) move any Supabase-specific policy adapters into a clearly named subfolder such as `src/shared/lib/governance/supabase/`; (3) keep `legacySupabaseFacade.ts` importing only from that governance location; (4) align README path references and gate command descriptions to the final folder. Validation commands: `rg -n "legacySupabasePolicy|deprecationPolicy|legacy_supabase_facade" README.md src`, `npm run check:contracts`.

findings.summary: Image form submission flow is split across wrappers that mostly shuttle the same wide state object.
evidence: `useFormSubmission` destructures and repackages a large prop set, writes a parallel `formStateRef`, and forwards nearly the same fields to `useTaskParamsBuilder` and `useSubmissionHandlers`.
`useSubmissionHandlers` mostly re-forwards those fields into `useSubmissionOrchestrator` and then re-wraps returned commands through `useSubmitHandler` and `usePromptQueueHandlers`.
`useSubmissionOrchestrator` defines another large input interface tied to `UseFormSubmissionProps`, showing orchestration depends on many leaf fields instead of a tighter domain object.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a single `SubmissionContext` (state + derived helpers + validators) produced once in `useFormSubmission`, and pass that context through strategy-style handlers (`managed`, `automated`, `queueExisting`) instead of re-threading individual fields across each hook layer.

findings.summary: Lightbox boundary uses over-wide contracts and repackaging indirection instead of cohesive feature APIs.
evidence: `MediaLightboxProps` contains a very large flat surface and `useBundledLightboxProps` repackages many of the same values into grouped objects, adding indirection without reducing the upstream contract.

`useSharedLightboxState` composes many concern-specific interfaces into one monolithic hook input and returns a deeply nested mega-result object spanning variants, navigation, references, shots, lineage, layout, and button props.

`useVideoEditing` combines UI mode state, selection geometry, validation rules, LoRA state, and task dispatch in one hook, reinforcing a broad 'do-everything' pattern around lightbox editing features.
findings.suggestion: Define explicit feature modules with narrow interfaces (e.g., `lightboxNavigation`, `variantOps`, `shotOps`, `videoEditSession`) and have `MediaLightbox` compose them via typed feature adapters rather than one aggregate prop bag and one aggregate state hook.

findings.summary: Timeline persistence orchestration is duplicated across core and frame-update hooks.
evidence: Both files define near-identical queue logging structure (`queued/start/end` branches), local `shortId` helpers, and write execution scaffolding around `runSerializedTimelineWrite` / timeout wrappers.
Both files implement similar persist-and-refresh choreography (write, sync metadata, invalidate/refetch caches) with slightly different local conventions, which increases drift risk.
The same persistence primitives (`persistTimelineFrameBatch`, legacy supabase writes, queue utilities) are orchestrated independently in each hook rather than behind one timeline write service.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared timeline write coordinator (queue logging, timeout behavior, cache invalidation contract, and failure handling) and make both hooks call that coordinator with operation-specific payloads only.

findings.summary: Supabase edge functions follow conflicting runtime/response patterns in the same subsystem.
evidence: Some functions use shared runtime/bootstrap helpers (`initEdgeRuntime`, shared `jsonResponse`, strict JSON parser helpers), while others still manually read env vars, create clients, and return raw `Response` objects.
`stripe-checkout` defines a local `jsonResponse` helper despite shared HTTP helpers existing in the same function suite.
`generate-upload-url` and `get-task-output` keep bespoke auth/body/response flow, creating parallel patterns for the same concerns (method guard, auth, parse, error mapping, response formatting).
findings.suggestion: Standardize all edge functions on a single request pipeline (bootstrap, method guard, auth, body parsing, response/error mapping) from `_shared`, and leave each function file responsible only for domain logic.
